findings.title: Potential Jenkins CVE: CVE-2011-4344
findings.description: A potential vulnerability (CVE-2011-4344) was found for Jenkins version 2.516.1. Please investigate further.
findings.recommendation: Upgrade Jenkins to the latest version and review the CVE details at https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2011-4344
findings.ai_recommendation: Update Jenkins to the latest LTS release immediately. Enable the Security Realm, disable anonymous access, and subscribe to the Jenkins Security Advisory feed. Use `--jenkins.model.Jenkins.disableRememberMe=true` and restrict agent-to-controller access via the built-in node security configuration.

findings.title: Exposed Jenkins Endpoint: login
findings.description: A potentially sensitive Jenkins endpoint was found at http://localhost:8080/login
findings.recommendation: Ensure that only authorized users have access to this endpoint. Review your Jenkins security configuration.
findings.ai_recommendation: Restrict Jenkins endpoint access: enable authentication, disable the CLI over Remoting, configure a reverse proxy to expose only required paths, and set `X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN` and `Content-Security-Policy` headers.
